title: When a WBS Shifts from Deliverables to Activities
summary: Describes the point in WBS decomposition when noun‑based deliverables become verb‑based activities, and how those activities feed the Define Activities process for scheduling.
content: During decomposition, the transition occurs when the lowest‑level WBS element can no longer be expressed as a noun and must be written as a verb phrase. At this stage each deliverable is split into specific tasks such as Design Frame, Create Prototype, or Build Frame. These verb‑based tasks become the activity list that serves as an input to the Define Activities process, enabling the Project Schedule, network diagrams, and cost estimates. If a deliverable cannot be linked to activities, it may be out of scope or insufficiently defined.
